tasks.title: Infrastructure setup: test/ dir, package.json scripts, helpers
tasks.description: Phase 1. Create top-level test/ directory and write test/helpers.js exporting reusable utilities: (1) stdout/stderr capture (monkey-patch process.stdout.write / console.log, return captured string, restore on cleanup); (2) temp XDG_CONFIG_HOME helper that creates a unique temp dir (node:os tmpdir + node:fs mkdtempSync) and returns {dir, cleanup}; (3) optional local node:http server helper that starts on an ephemeral port and returns {url, close}. Add scripts to package.json: "test": "node --test" and "test:coverage": "node --test --experimental-test-coverage". Acceptance: `npm test` runs node:test runner (zero exit even with no tests yet or with the suite), no new dependencies added to package.json. Use only built-in node: modules.

tasks.title: Enable testability: export parseFlags from src/cli.js
tasks.description: Phase 2. Make a minimal, additive change to src/cli.js so that parseFlags is exported via module.exports (e.g. module.exports.parseFlags = parseFlags, or add to existing exports object). Do NOT export or alter ensureKey or any interactive/process.exit/stdin code paths — keep them private. Do not change runtime behavior of the CLI. Acceptance: `require("../src/cli").parseFlags` resolves to the function; existing CLI behavior unchanged.

tasks.title: Util tests: checkResp, readData, maybeJson
tasks.description: Phase 3. Write test/util.test.js using node:test and node:assert/strict covering src/util.js. checkResp: 2xx response passes through; non-2xx throws an error carrying .status and .body. readData: covers its parsing/extraction behavior for expected inputs. maybeJson: returns parsed object for valid JSON, falls back to raw/string for non-JSON input. Use describe/test blocks. Acceptance: `npm test` passes; tests are mock-free and deterministic.

tasks.title: CLI parse tests: exhaustive parseFlags coverage
tasks.description: Phase 4. Write test/cli.test.js using node:test and node:assert/strict exhaustively exercising parseFlags exported from src/cli.js: long flags (--flag value), short flags (-f value), --key=value form, boolean flags, positional arguments, and mixed-order combinations. Assert resulting parsed structure for each case. Acceptance: `npm test` passes; covers each flag style with at least one explicit assertion.

tasks.title: Config round-trip tests under temp XDG_CONFIG_HOME
tasks.description: Phase 5a. Write test/config.test.js using node:test and node:assert/strict. CRITICAL: set process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME to a temp dir (via test/helpers.js) BEFORE requiring src/config.js, since config path is computed at module load. Cover load/save/clear round-trip (save then load returns same data; clear removes it) and legacy migration path if present. Clean up temp dir after. Acceptance: `npm test` passes; no writes to the real home/config dir.

tasks.title: UI tests: table() and color wrappers in non-TTY mode
tasks.description: Phase 5b. Write test/ui.test.js using node:test and node:assert/strict covering src/ui.js table() and color wrapper functions. Assert that in non-TTY mode output is plain (no ANSI escape codes) and that table() produces correctly formatted rows/columns. Ensure non-TTY by checking the module respects process.stdout.isTTY being falsy (the test runner is non-TTY). Acceptance: `npm test` passes; assertions confirm absence of ANSI codes and correct table layout.

tasks.title: Wire-up pre-commit hook and document coverage reality
tasks.description: Phase 6. Update the pre-commit hook (pre_commit in .jonggrang/jonggrang.json hooks, currently "echo no test command configured") to run the new test command `node --test`. Append to .jonggrang/progress.txt: the coverage reality (80% threshold intentionally not met by this pure-function first pass) and follow-up options (command-level mocking, local HTTP integration tests). Acceptance: pre-commit runs the suite; progress.txt records the gap and next layers.
